alternatives: Treat Aetheris as a CLI utility
Treat Aetheris as a generic agent library
consequences: All major subsystems require RFC/SPEC ownership
Quality gates become mandatory for production readiness
context: Aetheris is governed as an ASE-OS rather than a prompt wrapper.
decision: Preserve the kernel, knowledge, planning, execution, and intelligence layers as first-class platform subsystems.
future_revisions: Publish a versioned platform capability model
problem: Without an operating-system boundary the repo would collapse into isolated scripts and documentation fragments.
title: Aetheris Operates As An Autonomous Software Engineering Operating System
tradeoffs: Clearer ownership and stronger production posture

alternatives: Keep data only in transient memory
consequences: All durable knowledge must be typed and queryable
context: Downstream planning and execution need durable evidence.
decision: Use EKB objects and graph records as the durable knowledge plane.
future_revisions: Move EKB schemas into reusable schema files
problem: Recomputing or inferring knowledge on every step causes inconsistency and drift.
title: Engineering Knowledge Base Is The Durable Source Of Engineering Truth

context: The repo contains many engines with cross-layer dependencies.
decision: Require each engine to map to an RFC, SPEC, source module, public API, artifacts, tests, and telemetry.
problem: Undocumented module ownership creates orphan code and unsafe changes.
title: Every Subsystem Must Be Governed By RFC And SPEC Contracts

consequences: Future extraction must preserve public APIs and artifact contracts
context: SPEC-009 through SPEC-031 are currently implemented in a shared planning module.
decision: Document the monolith as an interim implementation boundary and require contract-safe extraction later.
future_revisions: Extract planners into dedicated modules behind compatibility imports
problem: Immediate physical decomposition would risk unrelated behavior churn.
title: Planning Engines Remain Monolithic Until Contract Decomposition Is Funded

consequences: Parallel batches must declare locked files
context: Autonomous execution requires safe task ordering.
decision: Use decomposed tasks, dependency graphs, topological ordering, critical paths, and file-lock-aware parallel batches.
problem: Linear execution wastes concurrency and unsafe parallel execution corrupts files.
title: Execution Uses DAG Scheduling And Bounded Parallelism

consequences: Interrupted runs must produce resume metadata
context: Autonomous execution can fail during code generation, review, or external operations.
decision: Persist checkpoints, classify failures, generate patch plans, and route git operations through explicit engine contracts.
future_revisions: Add full rollback drills to chaos tests
problem: Without checkpointing and recovery the platform loses work and cannot resume safely.
title: Recovery And State Persistence Are Mandatory Runtime Boundaries

consequences: Model choices must include confidence and cost evidence
context: Different engineering tasks require different context, quality, and cost profiles.
decision: Use model registries, prompt compilation, optimization, structured reasoning, and self-reflection.
future_revisions: Add live latency benchmarks when provider APIs are available
problem: Hard-coded model choice or loose prompts cause cost and quality drift.
title: Model And Prompt Intelligence Are Advisory, Measured, And Evidence-Gated
tradeoffs: Advisory routing may not force external tools

consequences: Claims require confidence and evidence references
context: Generated engineering guidance must remain grounded in repository evidence.
decision: Chunk, retrieve, rank, verify, and scan claims before release to execution.
future_revisions: Add hybrid search indexes and citation scoring
problem: Unsupported claims create hallucinated architecture and unsafe implementation steps.
title: Knowledge Retrieval And Verification Gate Engineering Claims

consequences: Governance generation becomes part of release prep
context: Governance must stay synchronized with the repository.
decision: Generate traceability, governance, ADR, drift, readiness, graph, metric, checklist, and report artifacts from source metadata.
future_revisions: Wire generation into CI quality gates
title: Enterprise Governance Artifacts Are Generated From Repository Evidence
tradeoffs: Repeatable outputs are easy to validate

consequences: Security controls must appear in docs, tests, and runbooks
context: Aetheris reads untrusted repositories and can generate code.
decision: Treat project content as untrusted evidence, redact secrets, enforce workspace boundaries, and fail closed on policy violations.
future_revisions: Add secret scanning and policy tests
problem: Secrets leakage, path traversal, prompt injection, and fabricated claims are systemic risks.
title: Security Is Fail-Closed Across Documentation And Runtime Contracts

consequences: Dashboards and readiness reviews consume generated metrics
context: Autonomous engineering needs measurable outcomes and debuggable failures.
decision: Every subsystem must emit structured status, duration, warning, artifact, and failure telemetry.
problem: Opaque automation cannot be operated in production.
title: Observability Is A First-Class Contract

consequences: Release readiness has a formal ARB verdict
context: The platform is moving toward enterprise operation.
decision: Score architecture, documentation, security, performance, scalability, maintainability, reliability, availability, recoverability, observability, testability, and operability with evidence and recommendations.
problem: A green unit suite is not sufficient for production approval.
title: Production Readiness Requires Evidence-Based Quality Gates
